concerns.text: T074 [P] runs Monte-Carlo validation as part of pipeline start-up, but T062 (Monte-Carlo validation module implementation) appears AFTER T074 in the task list. T074 depends on T062's output; consumer before producer violation.

T027 [P] end-to-end driver script orchestrates pipeline including power-analysis (T061) and Monte-Carlo validation (T062), but T061 and T062 appear AFTER T027. Driver script cannot call modules that don't exist yet.
T077 [P] extends manifest.json to include checksums from T076, but T077 is marked [P] (parallel-safe). T077 depends on T076's output (checksums.txt); cannot run in parallel with T076.
T047 [P] modifies run_audit.sh to invoke resource_monitor, but T046 (resource-monitoring module) must exist first. T047 is marked [P] but depends on T046's output.
T042 [P] validates manifest.json against schema, but T040 creates manifest.json. T042 is marked [P] but depends on T040's output; cannot run in parallel.

Contract tests (T014-T016) appear before implementation tasks (T017-T028). While TDD suggests tests first, these contract tests verify schema compliance of implementation artifacts that don't exist yet. Consider reordering or clarifying these are integration tests run after implementation.
Contract tests (T029-T030) appear before implementation tasks (T031-T037). Same issue as US1 - tests verify artifacts that don't exist yet.
Contract tests (T038-T039) appear before implementation tasks (T040-T044). Same ordering concern as above.
